meta.disclaimer: Informational overview only — not legal advice. Statuses reflect publicly available official information at the date shown and national implementation details vary. Always verify against the linked official sources.

statusLegend.implemented: Final national law transposing the Directive is in force.
statusLegend.in_progress: Draft legislation published, partial transposition in force, or a formal legislative process is underway.
statusLegend.not_yet: No published draft, or the transposition process is paused.

countries.name: Austria
countries.summary: No published draft or confirmed transposition activity. The 7 June 2026 deadline has passed without national implementing legislation.

countries.name: Belgium
countries.summary: Partial implementation exists in regional public-sector frameworks; federal transposition for the private sector is still under discussion. Belgium formally requested a six-month extension shortly before the deadline.

countries.name: Bulgaria
countries.summary: A draft transposition law went into public consultation in May–June 2026 but has not yet been adopted by parliament.

countries.name: Croatia
countries.summary: Implementation has been planned through amendments to the Labour Act, but no draft legislation has been published.

countries.name: Cyprus
countries.summary: A draft bill on equal remuneration was published by the Department of Labour Relations in late 2025 and has been through consultation, but no final law has been enacted.

countries.name: Czech Republic
countries.summary: A ban on pay-secrecy clauses has been in force since June 2025 as a partial step. A comprehensive Labour Code amendment was introduced in March 2026, targeting entry into force on 1 January 2027.

countries.name: Denmark
countries.summary: A draft bill has been published, openly acknowledging the missed deadline and proposing entry into force on 1 January 2027.

countries.name: Estonia
countries.summary: The government sought an EU-level postponement in April 2026, then signalled it would proceed with limited pre-employment transparency measures first while seeking to delay pay-gap reporting.

countries.name: Finland
countries.summary: A formal transposition process is underway but implementation has been delayed beyond the 7 June 2026 deadline.

countries.name: France
countries.summary: A draft transposition law has been published (expanded pay-gap indicators, comparator information rights, ban on pay-secrecy clauses) with a stated entry into force of 1 January 2027. It has not yet appeared in the Journal officiel.

countries.name: Germany
countries.summary: No draft bill yet, but an expert commission has published a detailed implementation model and a formal bill is expected. The existing Entgelttransparenzgesetz remains in force but falls short of the Directive's requirements.

countries.name: Greece
countries.summary: At an early preparatory stage; no draft implementing legislation has been published.

countries.name: Hungary
countries.summary: No published draft or confirmed transposition activity as of the last check.

countries.name: Ireland
countries.summary: The government has confirmed a phased transposition; published draft measures so far cover pre-employment transparency only, with further elements (including pay-gap reporting) to follow.

countries.name: Italy
countries.summary: Legislative Decree No. 96/2026 was published in the Gazzetta Ufficiale on 1 June 2026 and entered into force on 7 June 2026, covering recruitment transparency, information rights and reporting.

countries.name: Latvia
countries.summary: The Ministry of Welfare published a draft transposition law in spring 2026, broadly aligned with the Directive; it has not yet been enacted.

countries.name: Lithuania
countries.summary: The final transposition law was adopted in May 2026 with effect from 7 June 2026. Some provisions are staged: the right to information applies from 1 January 2027 and the first reporting deadline is March 2028.

countries.name: Luxembourg
countries.summary: No published draft or confirmed transposition activity as of the last check.

countries.name: Malta
countries.summary: Implementing regulations were published on 5 June 2026 and are in force, building on earlier partial measures that applied from August 2025.

countries.name: Netherlands
countries.summary: A comprehensive draft closely following the Directive has been published, but the government announced implementation is delayed to 1 January 2027, shifting the first reporting cycle accordingly.

countries.name: Poland
countries.summary: Recruitment-stage transparency rules are already in force. Broader draft legislation goes beyond the Directive, including reporting from 100 employees and a 30-day information-request deadline.

countries.name: Portugal
countries.summary: No published draft or confirmed transposition activity as of the last check.

countries.name: Romania
countries.summary: A revised draft transposition law was published in March 2026 and completed consultation in April; it has not yet been enacted.

countries.name: Slovakia
countries.summary: A comprehensive transposition law covering transparency and reporting requirements entered into force on 1 June 2026, meeting the Directive's deadline.

countries.name: Slovenia
countries.summary: No draft transposition legislation has been published. The existing Employment Relationships Act already contains an equal-pay principle, but it predates and falls short of the Directive.

countries.name: Spain
countries.summary: A prior public consultation on a transposing Royal Decree closed in May 2026, but no draft text has been published.

countries.name: Sweden
countries.summary: Transposition was paused in March 2026; the government is seeking renegotiation of the Directive at EU level, citing administrative burden and national labour-market arrangements.
